<content>1: # AgentCube CI/CD — Design
2: 
3: Sources: `/tmp/agentcube-ref/.github/workflows/`, `/tmp/agentcube-ref/test/`, and (for Make targets) `/tmp/agentcube-ref/Makefile`.
4: 
5: ## GitHub Actions workflows (summary table)
6: 
7: | Filename | Workflow `name` | Trigger events | Path filters (dorny/paths-filter or notes) | Jobs | Key steps | Env vars (job/step) | Secrets |
8: |----------|-----------------|----------------|---------------------------------------------|------|-----------|---------------------|---------|
9: | `main.yml` | Agentcube CI Workflow | `pull_request` → `main`, `release-*` | *(none)* | `build` | Checkout; Docker Buildx setup; verify Docker; `make docker-build` | — | — |
10: | `e2e.yml` | Agentcube E2E Tests | `pull_request` → `main`, `release-*` | *(none)* | `e2e-test` | Checkout; Go 1.23; `helm/kind-action@v1` **install_only** Kind v0.30.0, `cluster_name: agentcube-e2e`; `export ARTIFACTS_PATH=${{ github.workspace }}/e2e-logs`; `make e2e`; on failure upload `e2e-logs/`; `always` → `make e2e-clean` | `ARTIFACTS_PATH` (shell) | — |
11: | `lint.yml` | Lint | `pull_request` → `main`, `release-*` | `lint`: `**/*.go`, `go.mod`, `go.sum`, `.golangci.yml`, `.github/workflows/lint.yml` | `golangci` | Checkout; paths-filter; if changed: Go 1.24; `make lint` | — | — |
12: | `python-sdk-tests.yml` | Python SDK Tests | `pull_request`, `merge_group` | `sdk`: `sdk-python/**` | `python-sdk-tests` | Checkout; paths-filter; if changed: Python 3.12 in `sdk-python/`; `pip install pytest`; `pip install -e .`; `python -m pytest tests/ -v` | Default `working-directory: sdk-python` | — |
13: | `python-lint.yml` | Python Lint | `pull_request` → `main`, `release-*` | `python`: `cmd/cli/**`, `sdk-python/**`, `example/**`, `test/**/*.py`, `pyproject.toml`, workflow self | `python_lint` | Checkout; paths-filter; if changed: Python 3.10; `pip install ruff`; `ruff check . --config pyproject.toml` | — | — |
14: | `test-coverage.yml` | Test Coverage | `pull_request`, `merge_group`, `workflow_call` | `coverage`: `**` minus `*.md`, `*.svg`, `*.png` | `coverage` | Checkout; paths-filter; if changed: free disk space; Go 1.24; `go test` with coverage; step summary; Codecov upload; artifact `go-coverage` | `CODECOV_TOKEN` passed to Codecov action when present | `secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN` (optional; declared for `workflow_call`) |
15: | `codegen-check.yml` | Codegen Check | `pull_request` → `main`, `release-*` | `codegen`: `pkg/apis/**`, `hack/**`, `.github/workflows/**`, `Makefile` | `codegen-check` | Checkout; paths-filter; if changed: Go 1.24.4; `make gen-check` | — | — |
16: | `copyright-check.yml` | Copyright Check | `pull_request` → `main`, `release-*` | `copyright`: all files except `*.md`, `*.svg`, `*.png`, `docs/**`, `.github/**` | `build` | Checkout; paths-filter; if changed: `apt install moreutils`; `make gen-copyright`; `git diff --exit-code` | — | — |
17: | `codespell.yml` | Codespell | `pull_request` → `main`, `release-*` | *(none — scans tree after temporarily removing some manifests)* | `codespell` | Backup/remove `pyproject.toml`, `package.json`, `package-lock.json`; `pip install codespell`; `codespell` with `--check-filenames`, `--skip`, `--ignore-words-list`; restore files | — | — |
18: | `build-push-release.yml` | Build and Push Release Images | `push` → `main`; `push` tags `v*.*.*`, `v*.*.*-*` | *(none)* | `build-and-push` | Checkout; Go 1.24.4; Buildx; `docker/login-action` to `ghcr.io`; set `TAG` from ref; `make docker-buildx-push` (workload manager), `docker-buildx-push-router`, `docker-buildx-push-picod` with `IMAGE_REGISTRY` | `TAG`, `IMAGE_REGISTRY=ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}` | `GITHUB_TOKEN` (registry login) |
19: | `dify-plugin-publish.yml` | Dify Plugin Publish | `push` tags `dify-plugin/v*` | *(none)* | `publish` | Download `dify-plugin` CLI 0.0.6; install `yq`; read `manifest.yaml`; package plugin; checkout `author/dify-plugins` with token; move `.difypkg`; branch, commit, push; `gh pr create` to `langgenius/dify-plugins` | `GH_TOKEN` on PR step | `PLUGIN_ACTION` (repo checkout + `gh`; cross-repo PR) |
20: | `workflows-approve.yml` | Approve Workflows | `pull_request_target` types `labeled`, `synchronize` → `main`, `release-**` | *(logic: first-time contributor vs `ok-to-test` label)* | `approve` | `github-script`: list contributor PRs; if not first-time or has `ok-to-test`, list `action_required` workflow runs for SHA and approve | — | `GITHUB_TOKEN` |
21: 
22: ### Workflow notes
23: 
24: - **`e2e.yml`**: Uses Kind action with `install_only: true` so the cluster is created by `run_e2e.sh` (`kind create cluster`), avoiding double-creation.
25: - **`test-coverage.yml`**: `workflow_call` declares `CODECOV_TOKEN` optional for reusable invocation; PR runs use the repo secret when configured.
26: - **`build-push-release.yml`**: Uses older action major versions (`checkout@v3`, `setup-go@v4`, `buildx@v2`) than most other workflows.
27: 
28: ---
29: 
30: ## E2E test setup (`test/e2e/run_e2e.sh`)
31: 
32: ### Kind cluster
33: 
34: | Item | Value / behavior |
35: |------|------------------|
36: | Default cluster name | `E2E_CLUSTER_NAME` → `agentcube-e2e` |
37: | Creation | `kind create cluster --name "${E2E_CLUSTER_NAME}"` if missing; if exists and `E2E_CLEAN_CLUSTER=true` (default), delete then recreate |
38: | CI alignment | `.github/workflows/e2e.yml` installs Kind **v0.30.0** via `helm/kind-action@v1` (`install_only: true`) |
39: | Skip setup | `E2E_SKIP_SETUP=true` skips `run_setup` and assumes an existing cluster |
40: 
41: ### CRD / agent-sandbox installation
42: 
43: Applied via `kubectl apply --validate=false` after downloading release assets (URLs built from `AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION`, default **`v0.1.1`**):
44: 
45: 1. `https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox/releases/download/${AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION}/manifest.yaml`
46: 2. `https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox/releases/download/${AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION}/extensions.yaml`
47: 
48: ### Images pre-pulled and loaded into Kind
49: 
50: - `registry.k8s.io/agent-sandbox/agent-sandbox-controller:${AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION}`
51: - `python:3.9-slim` (echo agent workload)
52: 
53: Local images built and loaded:
54: 
55: - `make docker-build` → default `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE` **`workloadmanager:latest`**
56: - `make docker-build-router` → **`agentcube-router:latest`**
57: - `make docker-build-picod` → **`picod:latest`**
58: - `REDIS_IMAGE` default **`redis:7-alpine`** (Deployment + Service in namespace)
59: 
60: ### Helm (AgentCube chart)
61: 
62: | Item | Value |
63: |------|--------|
64: | Chart path | `manifests/charts/base` |
65: | Release / namespace | `agentcube` in `AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE` (default `agentcube`), `--create-namespace` |
66: | Redis addr | `redis.${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}.svc.cluster.local:6379`, password empty |
67: | Workload manager image | `repository=workloadmanager`, `tag=latest` |
68: | Router image | `repository=agentcube-router`, `tag=latest` |
69: | Router RBAC | `router.rbac.create=true`, `router.serviceAccountName=agentcube-router` |
70: | Extra env (JSON via `--set-json`) | Workload manager: `REDIS_PASSWORD_REQUIRED=false`, `JWT_KEY_SECRET_NAMESPACE=agentcube`; Router: `REDIS_PASSWORD_REQUIRED=false` |
71: | Wait | `helm upgrade --install ... --wait` |
72: 
73: ### Kubernetes fixtures applied during setup
74: 
75: | Path | Purpose |
76: |------|---------|
77: | `test/e2e/echo_agent.yaml` | `AgentRuntime` `echo-agent` in `agentcube` |
78: | Generated from `echo_agent.yaml` (sed) | `echo-agent-short-ttl` with `sessionTimeout: "30s"` for TTL tests |
79: | `test/e2e/e2e_code_interpreter.yaml` | `CodeInterpreter` `e2e-code-interpreter` in `agentcube` |
80: 
81: Warm-pool Go tests apply `e2e_code_interpreter_warmpool.yaml` at runtime (not in initial `kubectl apply` list in the shell script).
82: 
83: ### Port forwards and health checks
84: 
85: - Workload manager: `kubectl port-forward svc/workloadmanager` → `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT` (default **8080**) → pod 8080
86: - Router: `kubectl port-forward svc/agentcube-router` → `ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT` (default **8081**) → pod 8080
87: - Readiness: `curl` to `http://localhost:${WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT}/health` and `http://localhost:${ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT}/health/live`
88: 
89: ### Auth for tests
90: 
91: - ServiceAccount `e2e-test` in `agentcube` + `ClusterRoleBinding` to `workloadmanager` clusterrole
92: - API token: `kubectl create token e2e-test -n agentcube --duration=24h` → `API_TOKEN`
93: 
94: ### Python venv for E2E
95: 
96: - Directory: `E2E_VENV_DIR` (default `/tmp/agentcube-e2e-venv`)
97: - `python3 -m venv`, `pip install -e ./sdk-python`, verify `import agentcube`
98: 
99: ### Test commands invoked by `run_e2e.sh`
100: 
101: **Go** (from repository root):
102: 
103: ```bash
104: WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL="http://localhost:${WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT}" \
105: ROUTER_URL="http://localhost:${ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT}" \
106: API_TOKEN=$API_TOKEN \
107: go test -v ./test/e2e/...
108: ```
109: 
110: **Python** (after `cd` to `test/e2e`):
111: 
112: ```bash
113: WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL="..." ROUTER_URL="..." API_TOKEN=$API_TOKEN \
114: AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE="${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" \
115: "$E2E_VENV_DIR/bin/python" test_codeinterpreter.py
116: ```
117: 
118: (`test_codeinterpreter.py` uses `unittest.main()` and appends `--verbose` if missing.)
119: 
120: ### Failure artifacts
121: 
122: On test failure, `collect_component_logs` writes under `ARTIFACTS_PATH` (CI: `${{ github.workspace }}/e2e-logs`): workload manager, router, and sandbox pod logs/describes.
123: 
124: ---
125: 
126: ## Test directory structure
127: 
128: ```
129: test/
130: ├── OWNERS
131: └── e2e/
132:     ├── README.md
133:     ├── __init__.py
134:     ├── run_e2e.sh
135:     ├── e2e_test.go
136:     ├── test_codeinterpreter.py
137:     ├── echo_agent.yaml
138:     ├── e2e_code_interpreter.yaml
139:     └── e2e_code_interpreter_warmpool.yaml
140: ```
141: 
142: ### Fixture paths (relative to repo root unless noted)
143: 
144: | Fixture | Description |
145: |---------|-------------|
146: | `test/e2e/echo_agent.yaml` | Echo `AgentRuntime` for router invocation tests |
147: | `test/e2e/e2e_code_interpreter.yaml` | Standard `CodeInterpreter` (`e2e-code-interpreter`, namespace `agentcube`) |
148: | `test/e2e/e2e_code_interpreter_warmpool.yaml` | `CodeInterpreter` with `warmPoolSize: 2` in `default` (loaded by Go tests from **package cwd** as `e2e_code_interpreter_warmpool.yaml`) |
149: 
150: ---
151: 
152: ## Go test commands and flags in CI
153: 
154: | Workflow / entrypoint | Command | Flags / notes |
155: |------------------------|---------|----------------|
156: | `test-coverage.yml` | `go test` | `-race -v -coverprofile=coverage.out -coverpkg=./pkg/... ./pkg/...` |
157: | `e2e.yml` via `make e2e` → `run_e2e.sh` | `go test` | `-v ./test/e2e/...` with env `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL`, `ROUTER_URL`, `API_TOKEN` |
158: 
159: ---
160: 
161: ## Python test commands in CI
162: 
163: | Workflow | Working dir | Command |
164: |----------|-------------|---------|
165: | `python-sdk-tests.yml` | `sdk-python` | `python -m pip install --upgrade pip`; `pip install pytest`; `pip install -e .`; `python -m pytest tests/ -v` |
166: | `e2e.yml` (via `run_e2e.sh`) | Invoked from `test/e2e/` | `"$E2E_VENV_DIR/bin/python" test_codeinterpreter.py` (unittest, verbosity 2) |
167: 
168: ---
169: 
170: ## Key Make targets referenced by CI
171: 
172: | Target | Referenced in | Role |
173: |--------|----------------|------|
174: | `docker-build` | `main.yml`, `run_e2e.sh` | Build workload manager image |
175: | `docker-build-router` | `run_e2e.sh` | Build router image |
176: | `docker-build-picod` | `run_e2e.sh` | Build picod image |
177: | `docker-buildx-push` | `build-push-release.yml` | Push workload manager image (`IMAGE_REGISTRY`, `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE=workloadmanager:${TAG}`) |
178: | `docker-buildx-push-router` | `build-push-release.yml` | Push router image |
179: | `docker-buildx-push-picod` | `build-push-release.yml` | Push picod image |
180: | `e2e` | `e2e.yml` | Run `./test/e2e/run_e2e.sh` |
181: | `e2e-clean` | `e2e.yml` | `kind delete cluster --name $(E2E_CLUSTER_NAME)`; `rm -rf /tmp/agent-sandbox` |
182: | `lint` | `lint.yml` | `golangci-lint` (project default) |
183: | `gen-check` | `codegen-check.yml` | Verify generated code up to date |
184: | `gen-copyright` | `copyright-check.yml` | Generate copyright headers for check |
185: 
186: ---
187: 
188: ## Additional reference: E2E README vs script
189: 
190: `test/e2e/README.md` states Go **1.24+** and generic “install CRDs and agent-sandbox”; the **CI workflow** pins Go **1.23** for E2E, and **run_e2e.sh** pins **agent-sandbox `v0.1.1`** by default (overridable via `AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION`).

(End of file - total 190 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Session Routing Specification
2: 
3: ## Purpose
4: Route HTTP invocation traffic to sandboxes by session: resolve or create sessions via the workload manager, track activity in the store, reverse-proxy to pod endpoints, and sign outbound requests with JWT when targeting sandboxes.
5: 
6: ## Requirements
7: 
8: ### Requirement: Session resolution and creation
9: The system SHALL read session id from request header `x-agentcube-session-id`. When the header is empty, the system SHALL POST to the workload manager to create a sandbox. When non-empty, the system SHALL load `SandboxInfo` from the store by session id.
10: 
11: #### Scenario: Missing session triggers workload manager create
12: - **GIVEN** `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL` is set and an invocation request has no `x-agentcube-session-id`
13: - **WHEN** `GetSandboxBySession` runs for `AgentRuntimeKind`
14: - **THEN** the client POSTs JSON `{"kind":"AgentRuntime","name":...,"namespace":...}` to `{WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL}/v1/agent-runtime`
15: 
16: #### Scenario: Unknown session returns not found
17: - **GIVEN** a non-empty session id not present in store
18: - **WHEN** the store returns `ErrNotFound`
19: - **THEN** the error maps to `NewSessionNotFoundError(sessionID)` and the HTTP handler responds using API status (typically 404)
20: 
21: ### Requirement: Workload manager URL and auth token for creates
22: The system SHALL require environment variable `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL` at session manager construction (non-empty). When the file `/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token` exists and is readable, the system SHALL attach `Authorization: Bearer <trimmed contents>` to create requests.
23: 
24: #### Scenario: Missing WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL fails startup
25: - **GIVEN** `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL` is unset
26: - **WHEN** `NewSessionManager` is called
27: - **THEN** it returns error `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL environment variable is not set`
28: 
29: ### Requirement: Create response validation
30: The system SHALL accept workload manager HTTP 200 with body `CreateSandboxResponse`; SHALL reject with internal error if `sessionId` in body is empty; SHALL build in-memory `SandboxInfo` with `SandboxID`, `Name` (= `sandboxName`), `SessionID`, `EntryPoints` from the response (other fields may be zero).
31: 
32: #### Scenario: Non-OK from workload manager
33: - **GIVEN** workload manager returns HTTP 404
34: - **WHEN** the session manager handles the response
35: - **THEN** the error is `NewSandboxTemplateNotFoundError(namespace, name, kind)`
36: 
37: ### Requirement: Reverse proxy to sandbox
38: The system SHALL determine upstream URL from `SandboxInfo.EntryPoints` by first matching `path` prefix against the invocation path; if none match, SHALL use the first entry point. The system SHALL build URL by prefixing `protocol` (lowercase) and `://` to `endpoint` when protocol is set and endpoint lacks a scheme. The system SHALL use `httputil.NewSingleHostReverseProxy`, shared `http.Transport` on the proxy, and custom `Director` to set path, host, forwarding headers, and optional JWT.
39: 
40: #### Scenario: No entry points
41: - **GIVEN** `EntryPoints` is empty
42: - **WHEN** upstream is determined
43: - **THEN** the handler responds 404 JSON `{"error":"no entry point found for sandbox"}`
44: 
45: ### Requirement: JWT injection for sandbox kinds
46: For sandbox kinds `Sandbox` and `SandboxClaim`, the system SHALL generate a JWT before proxying and SHALL set `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on the proxied request.
47: 
48: #### Scenario: Signing failure
49: - **GIVEN** JWT generation returns an error
50: - **WHEN** forwarding is attempted
51: - **THEN** the handler responds 500 with JSON containing `"code":"JWT_SIGNING_FAILED"` and `"error":"failed to sign request"`
52: 
53: ### Requirement: Forwarding headers and response header
54: The system SHALL set `X-Forwarded-Host` from the incoming request host, `X-Forwarded-Proto` to `https` when TLS is present else `http`, and `X-Forwarded-For` by appending client IP to any existing values. The system SHALL set response header `x-agentcube-session-id` to the resolved session id on all proxied responses.
55: 
56: #### Scenario: Path normalization
57: - **GIVEN** the Gin path param is non-empty and lacks a leading slash
58: - **WHEN** the director runs
59: - **THEN** the outbound path is prefixed with `/`
60: 
61: ### Requirement: Proxy error mapping
62: The system SHALL map proxy errors containing `connection refused` to 502 `{"error":"sandbox unreachable"}`, containing `timeout` to 504 `{"error":"sandbox timeout"}`, and other errors to 502 `{"error":"sandbox unreachable"}`.
63: 
64: #### Scenario: Downstream connection refused
65: - **GIVEN** the reverse proxy fails with an error whose message includes `connection refused`
66: - **WHEN** `ErrorHandler` runs
67: - **THEN** status is 502 and error text is `sandbox unreachable`
68: 
69: ### Requirement: Last-activity updates
70: The system SHALL call `UpdateSessionLastActivity` with `time.Now()` after successfully resolving the sandbox and again after `forwardToSandbox` returns (twice per successful invoke path). Failures SHALL be logged as warnings only.
71: 
72: #### Scenario: Store update failure does not block proxy
73: - **GIVEN** `UpdateSessionLastActivity` returns an error
74: - **WHEN** handling continues
75: - **THEN** the proxy still runs if sandbox resolution succeeded
76: 
77: ### Requirement: Health endpoints
78: The system SHALL expose `GET /health/live` returning 200 `{"status":"alive"}`. The system SHALL expose `GET /health/ready` returning 200 `{"status":"ready"}` when session manager is non-nil, else 503 with `status` and `error` fields.
79: 
80: #### Scenario: Ready depends on session manager
81: - **GIVEN** `sessionManager` is nil
82: - **WHEN** `GET /health/ready` is served
83: - **THEN** status is 503 and body includes `"error":"session manager not available"`
84: 
85: ### Requirement: Concurrency limiting
86: The system SHALL apply a semaphore middleware to `/v1/*` routes with capacity `MaxConcurrentRequests` (default 1000 when config value ≤ 0). When no slot is available, the system SHALL respond 429 JSON `{"error":"server overloaded, please try again later","code":"SERVER_OVERLOADED"}` and abort.
87: 
88: #### Scenario: Default limit when zero in config
89: - **GIVEN** `NewServer` receives `MaxConcurrentRequests` 0
90: - **WHEN** the server is constructed
91: - **THEN** the effective limit is set to 1000 before route registration
92: 
93: ### Requirement: Invocation route surface
94: The system SHALL register GET and POST for `/v1/namespaces/:namespace/agent-runtimes/:name/invocations/*path` and for `/v1/namespaces/:namespace/code-interpreters/:name/invocations/*path`, with Gin logger, recovery, and concurrency middleware on the `/v1` group only.
95: 
96: #### Scenario: Kind passed to session lookup
97: - **GIVEN** agent-runtime invocation
98: - **WHEN** `handleInvoke` runs
99: - **THEN** kind argument is `AgentRuntime` (`types.AgentRuntimeKind`)
100: 
101: ### Requirement: HTTP server behavior
102: The system SHALL serve with `h2c` and `http2.Server`, `ReadTimeout` 30s, `IdleTimeout` 90s; on context cancel SHALL shutdown within 10s. Optional TLS SHALL require both cert and key paths.
103: 
104: #### Scenario: TLS misconfiguration
105: - **GIVEN** `EnableTLS` true and empty cert path
106: - **WHEN** `Start` runs
107: - **THEN** it returns error stating cert/key not provided
108: 
109: ### Requirement: Identity secret bootstrap for JWT
110: On server creation the system SHALL construct `JWTManager`, call `TryStoreOrLoadJWTKeySecret`, and fail server creation if that returns error (except in-cluster skip path returns nil when not in cluster).
111: 
112: #### Scenario: In-cluster secret creation
113: - **GIVEN** in-cluster config succeeds and secret does not exist
114: - **WHEN** `TryStoreOrLoadJWTKeySecret` runs
115: - **THEN** it creates opaque secret `picod-router-identity` in namespace from `AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE` or `default` with keys `private.pem` and `public.pem`

(End of file - total 115 lines)
</content>

# Session Routing Specification

## Purpose
Route HTTP invocation traffic to sandboxes by session: resolve or create sessions via the workload manager, track activity in the store, reverse-proxy to pod endpoints, and sign outbound requests with JWT when targeting sandboxes.

## Requirements

### Requirement: Session resolution and creation
The system SHALL read session id from request header `x-agentcube-session-id`. When the header is empty, the system SHALL POST to the workload manager to create a sandbox. When non-empty, the system SHALL load `SandboxInfo` from the store by session id.

#### Scenario: Missing session triggers workload manager create
- **GIVEN** `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL` is set and an invocation request has no `x-agentcube-session-id`
- **WHEN** `GetSandboxBySession` runs for `AgentRuntimeKind`
- **THEN** the client POSTs JSON `{"kind":"AgentRuntime","name":...,"namespace":...}` to `{WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL}/v1/agent-runtime`

#### Scenario: Unknown session returns not found
- **GIVEN** a non-empty session id not present in store
- **WHEN** the store returns `ErrNotFound`
- **THEN** the error maps to `NewSessionNotFoundError(sessionID)` and the HTTP handler responds using API status (typically 404)

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Session Routing — Design
2: 
3: ## Module
4: 
5: - Same repository: `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube` (see sandbox-orchestration design for `go.mod` and versions).
6: 
7: ## Package `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/router`
8: 
9: ### Config
10: 
11: ```go
12: const LastActivityAnnotationKey = "agentcube.volcano.sh/last-activity"
13: 
14: type Config struct {
15:     Port                  string
16:     Debug                 bool
17:     EnableTLS             bool
18:     TLSCert               string
19:     TLSKey                string
20:     MaxConcurrentRequests int  // 0 = unlimited at flag level; NewServer coerces to 1000
21: }
22: ```
23: 
24: ### CLI flags (`cmd/router/main.go`)
25: 
26: | Flag | Default |
27: |------|---------|
28: | `-port` | `8080` |
29: | `-enable-tls` | `false` |
30: | `-tls-cert` | `""` |
31: | `-tls-key` | `""` |
32: | `-debug` | `false` |
33: | `-max-concurrent-requests` | `1000` (0 means unlimited per flag help; `NewServer` still sets default 1000 when `<= 0`) |
34: 
35: ### Server struct
36: 
37: ```go
38: type Server struct {
39:     config         *Config
40:     engine         *gin.Engine
41:     httpServer     *http.Server
42:     sessionManager SessionManager
43:     storeClient    store.Store
44:     httpTransport  *http.Transport
45:     jwtManager     *JWTManager
46: }
47: ```
48: 
49: - `httpTransport`: `IdleConnTimeout: 0`, `DisableCompression: false`.
50: - Store: `store.Storage()` for both session manager and `storeClient`.
51: - Gin mode: `DebugMode` if `config.Debug`, else `ReleaseMode`.
52: 
53: ### Route table
54: 
55: | Method | Path | Middleware | Handler |
56: |--------|------|------------|---------|
57: | GET | `/health/live` | none | `handleHealthLive` |
58: | GET | `/health/ready` | none | `handleHealthReady` |
59: | GET | `/v1/namespaces/:namespace/agent-runtimes/:name/invocations/*path` | `gin.Logger`, `gin.Recovery`, `concurrencyLimitMiddleware` | `handleAgentInvoke` |
60: | POST | same | same | `handleAgentInvoke` |
61: | GET | `/v1/namespaces/:namespace/code-interpreters/:name/invocations/*path` | same | `handleCodeInterpreterInvoke` |
62: | POST | same | same | `handleCodeInterpreterInvoke` |
63: 
64: ### SessionManager interface
65: 
66: ```go
67: type SessionManager interface {
68:     GetSandboxBySession(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, namespace string, name string, kind string) (*types.SandboxInfo, error)
69: }
70: ```
71: 
72: ### manager implementation (`session_manager.go`)
73: 
74: ```go
75: type manager struct {
76:     storeClient     store.Store
77:     workloadMgrAddr string
78:     httpClient      *http.Client
79: }
80: ```
81: 
82: - `NewSessionManager(storeClient store.Store) (SessionManager, error)` reads `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL` (required).
83: - HTTP client: `Timeout: 2 * time.Minute`; transport `MaxIdleConnsPerHost: 100`, `DisableCompression: false`; `http2.ConfigureTransports` with `ReadIdleTimeout: 30s`, `PingTimeout: 15s`.
84: - Endpoints: `AgentRuntime` → `{WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL}/v1/agent-runtime`; `CodeInterpreter` → `.../v1/code-interpreter`.
85: - Request: `Content-Type: application/json`; optional `Authorization: Bearer` from `loadWorkloadManagerAuthToken()` reading `/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token` (trimmed); missing file → no header (warning if non-NotExist error).
86: 
87: ### JWT (`jwt.go`)
88: 
89: **Constants (unexported unless noted):**
90: 
91: | Name | Value |
92: |------|--------|
93: | `rsaKeySize` | `2048` |
94: | `jwtExpiration` | `5 * time.Minute` |
95: | `IdentitySecretName` | `"picod-router-identity"` |
96: | `PrivateKeyDataKey` | `"private.pem"` |
97: | `PublicKeyDataKey` | `"public.pem"` |
98: 
99: ```go
100: var IdentityNamespace = "default" // init: AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE if set
101: ```
102: 
103: **JWTManager:**
104: 
105: ```go
106: type JWTManager struct {
107:     privateKey *rsa.PrivateKey
108:     publicKey  *rsa.PublicKey
109:     clientset  kubernetes.Interface
110: }
111: ```
112: 
113: - `NewJWTManager()`: `rsa.GenerateKey(rand.Reader, rsaKeySize)`.
114: - `GenerateToken(claims map[string]interface{}) (string, error)`:
115:   - Base claims: `exp` = now+jwtExpiration (Unix), `iat` = now (Unix), `iss` = `"agentcube-router"`.
116:   - Merges custom claims (e.g. `session_id`).
117:   - Signing: `jwt.SigningMethodRS256`, signed with RSA private key.
118: 
119: **Algorithm:** RS256  
120: **Key type:** RSA 2048-bit, PKCS1 private key PEM (`RSA PRIVATE KEY`), PKIX public key PEM (`PUBLIC KEY`).
121: 
122: **TryStoreOrLoadJWTKeySecret(ctx):**
123: 
124: - If `clientset == nil`, try `rest.InClusterConfig()`; on failure log warning and return nil (ephemeral keys).
125: - Else build Secret `IdentitySecretName` in `IdentityNamespace`, type `Opaque`, labels `app=agentcube`, `component=router`, data `private.pem` + `public.pem`.
126: - Create; on `AlreadyExists`, Get secret and `loadPrivateKeyPEM`.
127: 
128: ### Reverse proxy
129: 
130: - `httputil.NewSingleHostReverseProxy(targetURL)`; `proxy.Transport = s.httpTransport`.
131: - Director: sets `req.URL.Path` to normalized path, clears `RawPath`, `req.Host = targetURL.Host`, forwarding headers, `Authorization` if JWT non-empty.
132: - `ModifyResponse`: sets `x-agentcube-session-id`.
133: - No per-request timeout wrapper (commented-out context timeout in source).
134: 
135: ### Concurrency middleware
136: 
137: - Buffered channel size `config.MaxConcurrentRequests`.
138: - `select`: send to channel or `default` → 429 + abort.
139: 
140: ### Health JSON shapes
141: 
142: - Live: `{"status":"alive"}`.
143: - Ready OK: `{"status":"ready"}`.
144: - Ready fail: `{"status":"not ready","error":"session manager not available"}`.
145: 
146: ### Error helper (`handleGetSandboxError`)
147: 
148: - If `apierrors.APIStatus`: use `Status().Code` if non-zero; message from status or `err.Error()`; if code is 500, replace message with `internal server error`; respond `gin.H{"error": message}`.
149: - Else 500 `{"error":"internal server error"}`.
150: 
151: ### Environment variables
152: 
153: | Variable | Used by |
154: |----------|---------|
155: | `WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL` | Session manager (required) |
156: | `AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE` | JWT secret namespace (optional, default `default`) |
157: 
158: ### Dependencies (direct use in router)
159: 
160: - `github.com/gin-gonic/gin`, `github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5`, `golang.org/x/net/http2`, `k8s.io/api`, `k8s.io/apimachinery`, `k8s.io/client-go`, `k8s.io/klog/v2`, `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/store`, `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/common/types`, `github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/pkg/api`.

(End of file - total 160 lines)
</content>

input.content:
# AgentCube Implementation Plan: AR-023 through AR-033

## Overview

This plan details the complete implementation of Architectural Requirements AR-023 through AR-033 for the AgentCube Kubernetes-native sandbox orchestration platform. Each AR results in complete, production-ready code with no placeholders or stubs.

## AR-023: CLI Toolkit Implementation

### Files to Create

1. **`cmd/cli/pyproject.toml`**
   - Package metadata for `agentcube` CLI
   - Dependencies: typer[all]>=0.9.0, pydantic>=2.0.0, pyyaml>=6.0, httpx>=0.24.0, docker>=6.0.0, rich>=13.0.0, packaging>=23.0, semver>=3.0.0
   - Console script: `kubectl-agentcube = "agentcube.cli.main:app"`
   - Optional dependencies for k8s, dev, test
   - Python >=3.10

2. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/__init__.py`**
   - `__version__ = "0.1.0"`
   - Exports: `app`, `PackRuntime`, `BuildRuntime`, `PublishRuntime`, `InvokeRuntime`, `StatusRuntime`
   - Imports from cli.main for app

3. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/cli/__init__.py`**
   - Empty package marker

4. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/cli/main.py`**
   - Typer app with global options `--version` (eager callback) and `--verbose`
   - `version_callback(value)` prints version and exits with `typer.Exit()`
   - Commands: `pack`, `build`, `publish`, `invoke`, `status`
   - Each command has `--verbose` local flag
   - `_handle_error(e, command_name, verbose)` prints red error, optionally traceback, exits 1
   - Success messages with Rich Progress spinners

5. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/models/pack_models.py`**
   - `MetadataOptions` dataclass with optional fields: agent_name, language, entrypoint, port, build_mode, requirements_file, description, workload_manager_url, router_url, readiness_probe_path, readiness_probe_port, registry_url, registry_username, registry_password, agent_endpoint
   - Class method `from_options(cls, options)` creates instance from dict

6. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/services/metadata_service.py`**
   - `AgentMetadata` Pydantic BaseModel with validators for language (python,java), build_mode (local,cloud), port (1-65535)
   - Fields: agent_name, description, language, entrypoint, port, build_mode, region, version, image, auth, requirements_file, registry_url, registry_username, registry_password, agent_endpoint, workload_manager_url, router_url, readiness_probe_path, readiness_probe_port, agent_id, session_id, k8s_deployment
   - `MetadataService` class with methods: load_metadata (fallback: agent_metadata.yaml > agent.yaml > metadata.yaml), save_metadata (yaml.dump with exclude_none), update_metadata, validate_workspace, _validate_python_workspace, _validate_java_workspace

7. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/services/docker_service.py`**
   - `DockerService` class using docker.from_env()
   - `check_docker_available()` returns bool
   - `build_image(dockerfile_path, context_path, image_name, tag, build_args)` - builds with optional buildargs, returns dict with image_name, image_id, image_size, build_time
   - `get_image_info(image_name)` - returns dict with id, size, created
   - `push_image(image_name, registry_url, username, password)` - login if credentials, retag, stream logs, returns pushed_image, push_time
   - `remove_image(image_name)` - returns bool, logs warning on failure

8. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/services/k8s_provider.py`**
   - `KubernetesProvider` class with kubernetes client
   - `__init__(namespace="default", verbose=False, kubeconfig=None)`
   - `_ensure_namespace()` creates if missing
   - `deploy_agent(agent_name, image_url, port, entrypoint=None, replicas=1, node_port=None, env_vars=None)` - creates Deployment and NodePort Service, waits for rollout, returns dict with deployment_name, service_name, namespace, replicas, container_port, node_port, service_url
   - `_create_deployment(name, image_url, port, entrypoint, replicas, env_vars)` - returns Deployment
   - `_create_service(name, port, node_port)` - returns Service
   - `wait_for_deployment_ready(name, timeout=120)` - raises TimeoutError if not ready
   - `get_agent_status(agent_name)` - returns status dict
   - `delete_agent(agent_name)` - deletes Deployment and Service, ignores 404
   - `_sanitize_name(name)` - converts to k8s-safe name

9. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/services/agentcube_provider.py`**
   - `AgentCubeProvider` class with kubernetes client
   - `__init__(namespace="default", verbose=False, kubeconfig=None)`
   - `_ensure_namespace()` creates if missing
   - `deploy_agent_runtime(agent_name, image_url, port, entrypoint=None, env_vars=None, workload_manager_url=None, router_url=None, readiness_probe_path=None, readiness_probe_port=None)` - creates AgentRuntime CRD with embedded WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL/ROUTER_URL if missing, sessionTimeout="15m", maxSessionDuration="8h", imagePullSecrets=[{"name":"default-secret"}]
   - `_sanitize_name(name)` - converts to k8s-safe name
   - `get_agent_runtime(name, namespace)` - returns CRD or None
   - CRD API: group="runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh", version="v1alpha1", plural="agentruntimes"

10. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/__init__.py`**
    - Re-exports: PackRuntime, BuildRuntime, PublishRuntime, InvokeRuntime, StatusRuntime, MetadataOptions

11. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/pack_runtime.py`**
    - `PackRuntime` class
    - `pack(workspace_path, **options)` - validates workspace, loads/creates metadata, applies CLI overrides, validates language compatibility, processes dependencies, optionally generates Dockerfile, updates pack metadata, returns dict
    - `_validate_workspace_structure(workspace_path)` - raises ValueError if not directory, Python requires *.py, Java requires pom.xml
    - `_load_or_create_metadata(workspace_path, options)` - loads existing or creates from options
    - `_apply_option_overrides(metadata, options)` - merges CLI options
    - `_validate_language_compatibility(workspace_path, metadata)` - delegates to _validate_python_compatibility or _validate_java_compatibility
    - `_validate_python_compatibility(workspace_path)` - checks for *.py files
    - `_validate_java_compatibility(workspace_path)` - checks for pom.xml
    - `_process_dependencies(workspace_path, metadata)` - delegates to language-specific processors
    - `_process_python_dependencies(workspace_path)` - copies requirements.txt if present
    - `_process_java_dependencies(workspace_path)` - handles pom.xml
    - `_generate_dockerfile(workspace_path, metadata)` - returns path to generated Dockerfile
    - `_generate_python_dockerfile(metadata)` - returns Dockerfile content string
    - `_generate_java_dockerfile(metadata)` - returns Dockerfile content string
    - `_update_pack_metadata(workspace_path, metadata, dockerfile_path)` - saves metadata
    - `_prepare_output_path(workspace_path, output_path)` - resolves output directory
    - `_infer_entrypoint(workspace_path, language)` - guesses entrypoint from main.py or similar

12. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/build_runtime.py`**
    - `BuildRuntime` class
    - `build(workspace_path, **options)` - validates prerequisites, increments version, builds local or cloud, updates metadata, returns dict
    - `_increment_version(workspace_path, metadata)` - patches version (X.Y.Z+1 or append .1), reverts on failure
    - `_validate_build_prerequisites(workspace_path)` - checks Docker availability for local build
    - `_build_local(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - uses DockerService.build_image
    - `_build_cloud(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - cloud build logic
    - `_update_build_metadata(workspace_path, metadata, build_result, tag="latest")` - saves image info

13. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/publish_runtime.py`**
    - `PublishRuntime` class
    - `publish(workspace_path, **options)` - validates, prepares image, publishes via AgentCubeProvider or KubernetesProvider, updates metadata, returns dict
    - `_publish_cr_to_k8s(workspace_path, metadata, **options)` - uses AgentCubeProvider.deploy_agent_runtime, wait_for_deployment_ready
    - `_publish_k8s(workspace_path, metadata, **options)` - uses KubernetesProvider.deploy_agent, wait_for_deployment_ready
    - `_prepare_image_for_publishing(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - returns image URL after push or explicit URL
    - `_prepare_local_image(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - pushes image via DockerService.push_image
    - `_prepare_cloud_image(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - cloud image preparation
    - `_update_publish_metadata(workspace_path, agent_info)` - saves deployment info

14. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/invoke_runtime.py`**
    - `InvokeRuntime` class
    - `invoke(workspace_path, payload, headers=None)` - validates, gets endpoint, POSTs JSON with session header, returns response
    - `_validate_invoke_prerequisites(workspace_path)` - loads metadata, validates published state, returns tuple (metadata, endpoint, provider)
    - `async def _invoke_agent_via_agentcube(agent_id, payload, headers, endpoint, workspace_path)` - if k8s_deployment.type=="AgentRuntime", rewrites URL to {base}/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/agent-runtimes/{agent_name}/invocations/, adds X-Agentcube-Session-Id header, makes async request
    - `async def _direct_http_invocation(endpoint, payload, headers, workspace_path)` - direct HTTP POST

15. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/status_runtime.py`**
    - `StatusRuntime` class
    - `get_status(workspace_path, provider=None)` - loads metadata, queries status via provider, displays Rich Table, returns dict
    - `_get_k8s_status(metadata)` - gets Deployment status via KubernetesProvider
    - `_get_cr_k8s_status(metadata)` - gets AgentRuntime CRD status via AgentCubeProvider

16. **`cmd/cli/agentcube/operations/__init__.py`**
    - Empty module with docstring placeholder

17. **`cmd/cli/examples/hello-agent/main.py`**
    - Simple HTTP server example agent

18. **`cmd/cli/examples/math-agent/main.py`**
    - Mathematical computation example agent

## AR-024: Session Store Implementation

### Files to Create

1. **`pkg/store/store.go`**
   - Store interface with methods: Ping, GetSandboxBySessionID, StoreSandbox, UpdateSandbox, DeleteSandboxBySessionID, ListExpiredSandboxes, ListInactiveSandboxes, UpdateSessionLastActivity, Close
   - ErrNotFound error constant
   - Storage() singleton function using sync.Once
   - initStore() function for lazy initialization

2. **`pkg/store/singleton.go`**
   - Constants: redisStoreType="redis", valkeyStoreType="valkey"
   - Variables: initStoreOnce, provider Store
   - Storage() calls initStoreOnce.Do(initStore)
   - initStore() reads STORE_TYPE env var, constructs Redis or Valkey store, calls klog.Fatalf on error

3. **`pkg/store/store_redis.go`**
   - redisStore struct with cli (*redisv9.Client), sessionPrefix="session:", expiryIndexKey="session:expiry", lastActivityIndexKey="session:last_activity"
   - NewRedisStore() reads REDIS_ADDR (required), REDIS_PASSWORD (required unless REDIS_PASSWORD_REQUIRED=false), creates client
   - makeRedisOptions() returns &redisv9.Options with Addr and Password
   - Ping(ctx) - issues PING, expects "PONG"
   - GetSandboxBySessionID(ctx, sessionID) - GET session:{id}, unmarshals to types.SandboxInfo, returns ErrNotFound on nil
   - StoreSandbox(ctx, sandboxStore) - validates non-nil and non-zero ExpiresAt, pipeline: SETNX session:{id}, ZADD session:expiry, ZADD session:last_activity with time.Now().Unix()
   - UpdateSandbox(ctx, sandboxStore) - SET with XX flag, error if key doesn't exist
   - DeleteSandboxBySessionID(ctx, sessionID) - pipeline: DEL session:{id}, ZREM session:expiry, ZREM session:last_activity
   - ListExpiredSandboxes(ctx, before, limit) - ZRANGEBYSCORE session:expiry -inf {before.Unix} LIMIT 0 limit, pipeline GETs, returns []*types.SandboxInfo
   - ListInactiveSandboxes(ctx, before, limit) - ZRANGEBYSCORE session:last_activity -inf {before.Unix} LIMIT 0 limit, pipeline GETs
   - UpdateSessionLastActivity(ctx, sessionID, at) - validates sessionID, GET to check existence, ZADD with at or now if zero, returns ErrNotFound if missing
   - Close() - cli.Close()
   - Helper: sessionKey(id) returns sessionPrefix + sessionID
   - Helper: loadSandboxesBySessionIDs(ctx, sessionIDs) - pipeline GET, skips missing keys

4. **`pkg/store/store_valkey.go`**
   - valkeyStore struct with cli (valkey.Client), same string constants as Redis
   - NewValkeyStore() reads VALKEY_ADDR (comma-separated), VALKEY_PASSWORD, VALKEY_DISABLE_CACHE, VALKEY_FORCE_SINGLE, creates client
   - makeValkeyOptions() returns &valkey.ClientOption with InitAddress, Password, DisableCache, ForceSingleClient
   - Ping(ctx) - B().Ping(), expects "PONG"
   - GetSandboxBySessionID(ctx, sessionID) - B().Get().Key(key), IsValkeyNil → ErrNotFound, unmarshals
   - StoreSandbox(ctx, sandboxStore) - DoMulti: SETNX, ZADD expiry, ZADD last_activity
   - UpdateSandbox(ctx, sandboxStore) - SET ... XX, success requires ToString()=="OK"
   - DeleteSandboxBySessionID(ctx, sessionID) - DoMulti: DEL, ZREM expiry, ZREM last_activity
   - ListExpiredSandboxes(ctx, before, limit) - Zrangebyscore with Min("-inf"), Max(fmt.Sprintf("%d", before.Unix())), Limit(0, limit), then loadSandboxesBySessionIDs
   - ListInactiveSandboxes(ctx, before, limit) - same on last_activity key
   - UpdateSessionLastActivity(ctx, sessionID, at) - Exists key → int64 must be 1, then Zadd with at.Unix()
   - Close() - vs.cli.Close()
   - loadSandboxesBySessionIDs(ctx, sessionIDs) - builds all session:{id} keys, single Mget
   - ZSET scores: expiry = float64(sandboxRedis.ExpiresAt.Unix()), last_activity = float64(time.Now().Unix()) or at.Unix()

## AR-025: Integrations Implementation

### Files to Create

1. **`integrations/dify-plugin/main.py`**
   - Plugin process entry point
   - `if __name__ == "__main__": Plugin(DifyPluginEnv(MAX_REQUEST_TIMEOUT=120)).run()`

2. **`integrations/dify-plugin/manifest.yaml`**
   - version: 0.0.2
   - type: plugin
   - author: volcano-sh
   - name: agentcube
   - label: multilingual map (en_US, ja_JP, zh_Hans, pt_BR)
   - description: multilingual strings
   - icon: icon.png
   - icon_dark: icon-dark.png
   - resource: memory, permission subtree (tool, model, endpoint, app, storage)
   - plugins.tools: [provider/agentcube.yaml]
   - meta: version, arch (amd64, arm64), runner (language: python, version: "3.12", entrypoint: main), minimum_dify_version: null

3. **`integrations/dify-plugin/provider/agentcube.yaml`**
   - identity: author, name, label (multilingual), description (multilingual), icon
   - tools: [tools/agentcube-code-interpreter.yaml]
   - extra.python.source: provider/agentcube.py

4. **`integrations/dify-plugin/provider/agentcube.py`**
   - `from dify_plugin import ToolProvider`
   - `from dify_plugin.errors.tool import ToolProviderCredentialValidationError`
   - `class AgentcubeCodeInterpreterProvider(ToolProvider):`
     - `_validate_credentials(self, credentials)` - validates credentials, raises ToolProviderCredentialValidationError on failure

5. **`integrations/dify-plugin/tools/agentcube-code-interpreter.yaml`**
   - identity.name: agentcube-code-interpreter
   - identity.author: agentcube
   - identity.label/description: human + llm strings
   - parameters with name, type, required, label, human_description, llm_description, form:
     - router_url (string, required, form)
     - workload_manager_url (string, required, form)
     - language (select, optional, llm): python, javascript, typescript
     - code (string, optional, llm)
     - command (string, optional, llm)
     - session_id (string, optional, llm)
     - session_reuse (boolean, optional, llm)
     - code_interpreter_id (string, optional, llm)
   - extra.python.source: tools/agentcube-code-interpreter.py

6. **`integrations/dify-plugin/tools/agentcube-code-interpreter.py`**
   - `from collections.abc import Generator`
   - `from typing import Any`
   - `from dify_plugin import Tool`
   - `from dify_plugin.entities.tool import ToolInvokeMessage`
   - `from agentcube import CodeInterpreterClient`
   - `class AgentcubeCodeInterpreterTool(Tool):`

## AR-027: Code Execution (PicoD) Daemon

### Files to Create

1. **`pkg/picod/config.go`**
   - Config struct with Port (int, default 8080), Workspace (string, default "" → resolved to CWD)

2. **`pkg/picod/server.go`**
   - Server struct with engine (*gin.Engine), config Config, authManager *AuthManager, startTime time.Time, workspaceDir string
   - NewServer(config Config) - initializes gin.New(), sets ReleaseMode, uses Logger() and Recovery() middleware, loads public key via AuthManager.LoadPublicKeyFromEnv() (fatal on error), sets startTime, resolves workspaceDir
   - Run() - creates http.Server with Addr fmt.Sprintf(":%d", config.Port), Handler engine, ReadHeaderTimeout 10s, ListenAndServe
   - handleHealthCheck - GET /health, returns JSON {"status":"ok", "service":"PicoD", "version":"0.0.1", "uptime": time.Since(s.startTime).String()}
   - Routes: /api group with AuthMiddleware, handlers ExecuteHandler, UploadFileHandler, ListFilesHandler, DownloadFileHandler

3. **`pkg/picod/execute.go`**
   - ExecuteRequest struct with Command []string, Timeout string, WorkingDir string, Env map[string]string
   - ExecuteResponse struct with Stdout, Stderr, ExitCode, Duration (float64), StartTime, EndTime
   - Constant TimeoutExitCode = 124
   - ExecuteHandler - POST /api/execute, binds JSON, validates non-empty Command, parses Timeout with time.ParseDuration (default 60s), resolves WorkingDir via sanitizePath, creates exec.CommandContext with timeout, captures stdout/stderr to bytes.Buffer, returns ExecuteResponse
   - On context.DeadlineExceeded → exit_code = TimeoutExitCode, stderr includes timeout message
   - On invalid timeout → HTTP 400 with error describing invalid format
   - On empty command → HTTP 400 with "command cannot be empty"

4. **`pkg/picod/files.go`**
   - FileInfo struct with Path, Size, Mode (string), Modified
   - UploadFileRequest struct with Path, Content (base64), Mode
   - FileEntry struct with Name, Size, Modified, Mode (string), IsDir
   - ListFilesResponse struct with Files []FileEntry
   - Constant maxFileMode = 0777
   - parseFileMode(modeStr) - parses octal mode, clamps to maxFileMode, returns 0644 on invalid
   - UploadFileHandler - POST /api/files, checks Content-Type prefix multipart/form-data
     - Multipart: reads path form field, file form field, optional mode form field, creates parent dirs (0755), writes file stream with parseFileMode mode (default 0644), returns FileInfo
     - JSON: binds UploadFileRequest, decodes base64 Content, creates dirs, writes file, returns FileInfo
   - On missing path/file → HTTP 400
   - On invalid base64 → HTTP 400
   - ListFilesHandler - GET /api/files?path=..., validates path non-empty, sanitizes, os.ReadDir, returns ListFilesResponse, skips entries where Info() fails
   - On missing path param → HTTP 400 with "Missing 'path' query parameter"
   - On directory not found → HTTP 404 with "Directory not found"
   - DownloadFileHandler - GET /api/files/*path, extracts path param, trims leading /, sanitizes via sanitizePath, validates is regular file (not directory), sets headers: Content-Description, Content-Transfer-Encoding, Content-Disposition, Content-Type from mime.TypeByExtension or application/octet-stream, streams with c.File
   - On path escape → HTTP 400 with access denied
   - On is directory → HTTP 400 with "Path is a directory, not a file"
   - On not found → HTTP 404 with "File not found"

5. **`pkg/picod/auth.go`**
   - AuthManager struct with publicKey *rsa.PublicKey, mutex sync.RWMutex
   - Constant PublicKeyEnvVar = "PICOD_AUTH_PUBLIC_KEY", MaxBodySize = 32 << 20 (32 MiB)
   - LoadPublicKeyFromEnv() - reads env var, PEM decode, x509.ParsePKIXPublicKey, asserts *rsa.PublicKey
   - AuthMiddleware - requires Authorization header, expects "Bearer <token>", validates via jwt.Parse with RS256, WithExpirationRequired, WithIssuedAt, WithLeeway(1 minute), wraps body with http.MaxBytesReader(c.Writer, c.Request.Body, MaxBodySize)
   - On missing/malformed header → HTTP 401 JSON with error, code, detail
   - On invalid/expired token → HTTP 401 with "Invalid token", code, detail
   - On missing public key at startup → klog.Fatalf, no serve

6. **`pkg/picod/sanitize.go`**
   - sanitizePath(p) - validates workspaceDir initialized, resolves workspace via EvalSymlinks/Abs/Clean, cleans input path, strips leading separator if absolute, joins with workspace, cleans again, checks filepath.Rel for ".." escape, re-checks with EvalSymlinks if exists, returns safe path

7. **`cmd/picod/main.go`**
   - Flags: -port (int, 8080), -workspace (string, "")
   - klog.InitFlags(nil)
   - Constructs picod.Config, picod.NewServer(config), server.Run()

    - class CodeInterpreterDataPlaneClient:
      - __init__(self, session_id, router_url=None, namespace=None, cr_name=None, base_url=None, timeout=120, connect_timeout=5.0, pool_connections=10, pool_maxsize=10)
        - resolves base_url from base_url or urljoin(router_url, "/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/code-interpreters/{cr_name}/invocations/")
        - creates session with create_session
        - sets default session header: x-agentcube-session-id: {session_id}
      - _request(self, method, endpoint, body=None, **kwargs) -> requests.Response:
        - sets headers: Content-Type: application/json if body, x-agentcube-session-id
        - returns response
      - execute_command(self, command, timeout=None) -> str:
        - POST api/execute with JSON {"command": [cmd] if str else cmd, "timeout": "<n>s"}
        - read timeout = timeout_value + 2.0 if numeric else self.timeout
        - returns stdout
        - raises CommandExecutionError on non-zero exit_code
      - run_code(self, language, code, timeout=None) -> str:
        - if language in [python, py, python3]: writes timestamped .py file, executes python3 file
      - write_file(self, content, remote_path):
        - POST api/files with JSON {"path": remote_path, "content": base64.b64encode(content).decode(), "mode": "0644"}
      - upload_file(self, local_path, remote_path):
        - POST api/files multipart with files={'file': open(local_path,'rb')}, data={'path': remote_path, 'mode': '0644'}, headers={'x-agentcube-session-id': session_id}
      - download_file(self, remote_path, local_path):
        - GET api/files/{clean_path} with stream=True, writes to local_path
      - list_files(self, path="."):
        - GET api/files?path={path}, returns JSON with files array
      - close(self):
        - closes session

11. **`sdk-python/agentcube/clients/agent_runtime_data_plane.py`**
    - class AgentRuntimeDataPlaneClient:
      - SESSION_HEADER = "x-agentcube-session-id"
      - __init__(self, router_url, namespace, agent_name, timeout=120, connect_timeout=5.0, pool_connections=10, pool_maxsize=10)
        - base_url = urljoin(router_url, f"/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/agent-runtimes/{agent_name}/invocations/")
        - creates session with create_session
      - bootstrap_session_id(self) -> str:
        - GET base_url, reads header x-agentcube-session-id
        - raises ValueError if missing
      - invoke(self, session_id, payload, timeout=None) -> requests.Response:
        - POST base_url with json=payload, headers={SESSION_HEADER: session_id, "Content-Type": "application/json"}
        - timeout=(connect_timeout, timeout or self.timeout)
      - close(self):
        - closes session

12. **`sdk-python/agentcube/code_interpreter.py`**
    - class CodeInterpreterClient:
      - __init__(self, name="my-interpreter", namespace="default", ttl=3600, workload_manager_url=None, router_url=None, auth_token=None, verbose=False, session_id=None)
        - validates router_url provided or ROUTER_URL env var
        - creates ControlPlaneClient with workload_manager_url, auth_token
        - if session_id not provided: calls create_session via cp_client, sets session_id
        - calls _init_data_plane() to create CodeInterpreterDataPlaneClient
        - on _init_data_plane failure: warns, calls delete_session, re-raises
      - _init_data_plane(self):
        - creates CodeInterpreterDataPlaneClient with session_id, router_url, namespace, name
      - __enter__(self):
        - returns self
      - __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
        - calls stop()
        - returns False (don't suppress exceptions)
      - stop(self):
        - closes dp_client
        - if session_id was set (not provided): calls delete_session
        - closes cp_client
      - execute_command(self, command, timeout=None) -> str:
        - delegates to dp_client.execute_command
        - raises CommandExecutionError on non-zero exit
      - run_code(self, language, code, timeout=None) -> str:
        - delegates to dp_client.run_code
      - write_file(self, content, remote_path):
        - delegates to dp_client.write_file
      - upload_file(self, local_path, remote_path):
        - delegates to dp_client.upload_file
      - download_file(self, remote_path, local_path):
        - delegates to dp_client.download_file
      - list_files(self, path="."):
        - delegates to dp_client.list_files

13. **`sdk-python/agentcube/agent_runtime.py`**
    - class AgentRuntimeClient:
      - __init__(self, agent_name, namespace="default", router_url=None, verbose=False, session_id=None, timeout=120, connect_timeout=5.0)
        - validates router_url provided or ROUTER_URL env var
        - creates AgentRuntimeDataPlaneClient with router_url, namespace, agent_name, timeout, connect_timeout
        - if session_id not provided: calls bootstrap_session_id() via dp_client, sets session_id
        - raises ValueError if bootstrap fails
      - __enter__(self):
        - returns self
      - __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
        - calls close()
        - returns False
      - invoke(self, payload, timeout=None) -> Any:
        - validates session_id non-empty, raises ValueError if not
        - calls dp_client.invoke(session_id, payload, timeout)
        - returns response.json()
      - close(self):
        - closes dp_client

## AR-030: Idle Cleanup Controller

### Files to Create

1. **`pkg/agentd/reconciler.go`**
   - var SessionExpirationTimeout = 15 * time.Minute
   - type Reconciler struct with embed controller-runtime.Client, Scheme *runtime.Scheme
   - Reconcile(ctx, req) -> ctrl.Result, error:
     - gets Sandbox from API
     - if not found → return ctrl.Result{}, nil
     - reads last-activity-time annotation from workloadmanager.LastActivityAnnotationKey
     - if exists and non-empty:
       - parses with time.RFC3339
       - on parse error → return ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: 30s}, err
       - expirationTime = lastActivity.Add(SessionExpirationTimeout)
       - if time.Now().After(expirationTime):
         - deletes Sandbox
         - on NotFound → return ctrl.Result{}, nil
         - on other error → return ctrl.Result{}, err
       - else:
         - return ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: time.Until(expirationTime)}, nil
     - else:
       - return ctrl.Result{}, nil
   - SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error:
     - returns ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).For(&sandboxv1alpha1.Sandbox{}).Complete(r)

2. **`cmd/agentd/main.go`**
   - var schemeBuilder = runtime.NewScheme()
   - func init():
     - utilruntime.Must(scheme.AddToScheme(schemeBuilder))
     - utilruntime.Must(sandboxv1alpha1.AddToScheme(schemeBuilder))
   - mgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{
     - Scheme: schemeBuilder,
     - Metrics: metricsserver.Options{BindAddress: "0"},
     - HealthProbeBindAddress: "0",
   })
   - err = ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).For(&sandboxv1alpha1.Sandbox{}).Complete(&agentd.Reconciler{
     - Client: mgr.GetClient(),
     - Scheme: mgr.GetScheme(),
   })
   - mgr.Start(ctrl.SetupSignalHandler())

   - NewServer(config) - initializes gin with DebugMode if config.Debug else ReleaseMode, sets routes, creates JWTManager, calls TryStoreOrLoadJWTKeySecret

   - SessionManager interface: GetSandboxBySession(ctx, sessionID, namespace, name, kind) -> (*types.SandboxInfo, error)
   - manager struct with storeClient store.Store, workloadMgrAddr string, httpClient *http.Client
   - NewSessionManager(storeClient) (SessionManager, error):
     - reads WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL (required), returns error if unset
     - creates httpClient with Timeout: 2m, MaxIdleConnsPerHost: 100, http2.ConfigureTransports
     - loads auth token from /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
   - GetSandboxBySession(ctx, sessionID, namespace, name, kind):
     - if sessionID empty: POST to {workloadMgrAddr}/v1/agent-runtime or /v1/code-interpreter with JSON {"kind", "name", "namespace"}, returns CreateSandboxResponse with SandboxInfo
     - else: storeClient.GetSandboxBySessionID, returns ErrNotFound on miss
     - on workload manager 404: returns NewSandboxTemplateNotFoundError
     - validates sessionId non-empty in response

   - const rsaKeySize = 2048, jwtExpiration = 5*time.Minute
   - const IdentitySecretName = "picod-router-identity", PrivateKeyDataKey = "private.pem", PublicKeyDataKey = "public.pem"
   - var IdentityNamespace = "default" (init from AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE)
   - JWTManager struct with privateKey *rsa.PrivateKey, publicKey *rsa.PublicKey, clientset kubernetes.Interface
   - NewJWTManager() - rsa.GenerateKey(rand.Reader, rsaKeySize)
   - GenerateToken(claims map[string]interface{}) (string, error):
     - base claims: exp=now+jwtExpiration, iat=now, iss="agentcube-router"
     - merges custom claims
     - signs with RS256
   - TryStoreOrLoadJWTKeySecret(ctx):
     - if clientset nil: tries in-cluster config, returns nil on failure (ephemeral)
     - else: creates secret IdentitySecretName in IdentityNamespace with data private.pem, public.pem

     - custom ModifyResponse: sets x-agentcube-session-id header
     - ErrorHandler: maps errors (connection refused, timeout) to 502/504 JSON
     - on JWT signing error: 500 JSON {"code":"JWT_SIGNING_FAILED","error":"failed to sign request"}
     - on no entry points: 404 JSON {"error":"no entry point found for sandbox"}

## Implementation Notes

- All Go code must use proper error handling with context
- All HTTP handlers must return appropriate status codes with JSON error messages
- All Kubernetes operations must use proper client-go patterns with informers and listers
- All Python code must follow PEP 8 and include type hints
- All Docker images must run as non-root users except PicoD which runs as root for chattr
- All Helm templates must use proper Go template syntax with safe defaults
- All CI/CD workflows must use proper GitHub Actions best practices
- All tests must be written and pass before considering implementation complete

## Summary

This plan implements all 11 Architectural Requirements (AR-023 through AR-033) with complete, production-ready code covering:
- Full Python CLI toolkit for agent lifecycle management
- Redis/Valkey session store with atomic operations
- Dify plugin integration and PCAP analyzer example
- Complete deployment infrastructure (Helm, Docker, Makefile)
- PicoD HTTP daemon for jailed code execution
- Sandbox orchestration control plane (CRDs, workload manager)
- Python SDK with control and data plane clients
- Idle cleanup controller for sandbox garbage collection
- Full CI/CD pipeline with tests and image publishing
- Session routing service with JWT authentication
- Complete CRD definitions and generated code

Each file will contain full implementation with no placeholders or stubs, ensuring the system is production-ready and fully functional.

339:   - `ListInactiveSandboxes()` - ZRANGEBYSCORE session:last_activity -inf before LIMIT 0 limit, then GETs

354:   - `ListExpiredSandboxes()` - Zrangebyscore Min("-inf"), Max(before.Unix()), Limit(0, limit), then MGET

603: ## AR-007: Idle Cleanup
604: 
605: ### Purpose
606: Run a controller that deletes Sandbox custom resources when an idle-timeout annotation indicates the sandbox has been inactive beyond a fixed grace period.
607: 
608: ### Files to Create
609: 
610: #### Main Entry Point
611: - `cmd/agentd/main.go`
612:   - No CLI flags
613:   - Create controller-runtime Manager with:
614:     - Scheme containing corev1 and sandboxv1alpha1
615:     - MetricsBindAddress "0"
616:     - HealthProbeBindAddress "0"
617:   - Register Reconciler for Sandbox
618:   - Start manager with SetupSignalHandler
619: 
620: #### Reconciler
621: - `pkg/agentd/reconciler.go`
622:   - `Reconciler` struct with Client, Scheme
623:   - `Reconcile(ctx, req)` - reconciles Sandbox:
624:     1. Get Sandbox by NamespacedName
625:     2. If NotFound → return empty Result, nil
626:     3. If other error → return Result, err
627:     4. Read last-activity-time annotation
628:     5. If missing or empty → return empty Result, nil
629:     6. Parse with time.RFC3339
630:     7. If parse error → return Result{RequeueAfter: 30s}, err
631:     8. Compute expiration = lastActivity + SessionExpirationTimeout
632:     9. If now.After(expiration) → Delete sandbox
633:     10. Else → return Result{RequeueAfter: time.Until(expiration)}, nil
634:   - `SetupWithManager(mgr)` - registers controller for Sandbox
635:   - `SessionExpirationTimeout = 15 * time.Minute`
636:   - Uses `workloadmanager.LastActivityAnnotationKey` = "last-activity-time"
637:

953:     - EXPOSE 8080
954:     - ENTRYPOINT ["/app/workloadmanager"]
955:     - CMD ["--port=8080"]
956: 
957: - `docker/Dockerfile.router`
958:   - Similar pattern, output binary: agentcube-router from ./cmd/router
959:   - Runtime user: router UID 1000
960:   - CMD ["--port=8080", "--debug"]
961: 
962: - `docker/Dockerfile.picod`
963:   - Stage builder: golang:1.24.4
964:     - WORKDIR /app

966:     - go mod download
967:     - COPY . .
968:     - CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./cmd/picod
969:   - Stage runtime: ubuntu:24.04
970:     - apt-get install -y python3
971:     - WORKDIR /root/
972:     - COPY --from=builder /app/picod
973:     - ENTRYPOINT ["./picod"]
974: 
975: #### Makefile

977:   - Variables: HUB, TAG, GOBIN, LOCALBIN, CONTROLLER_GEN, GOLANGCI_LINT, WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE, ROUTER_IMAGE, PICOD_IMAGE, IMAGE_REGISTRY, E2E_CLUSTER_NAME
978:   - Targets:

987:     - `build-agentd` - generate + go build bin/agentd

996:     - `docker-build-router` - docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.router -t $(ROUTER_IMAGE)

1006: #### Hack Scripts

1018: - `hack/boilerplate.go.txt` - Go file header template

1020: 
1021: ---
1022: 
1023: ## AR-010: Dify Plugin Integration
1024: 
1025: ### Purpose
1026: Dify-compatible Python plugin that provides AgentCube code interpreter as a tool for Dify.

1028: ### Files to Create

1053: - `integrations/dify-plugin/provider/agentcube.py`

1058: - `integrations/dify-plugin/tools/agentcube-code-interpreter.yaml`

1073: - `integrations/dify-plugin/tools/agentcube-code-interpreter.py`

1080: - `integrations/dify-plugin/requirements.txt`

1083: 
1084: - `integrations/dify-plugin/GUIDE.md` - Plugin usage guide
1085: - `integrations/dify-plugin/README.md` - README

1088: 
1089: ---
1090: 
1091: ## AR-011: PCAP Analyzer Example
1092: 
1093: ### Purpose
1094: FastAPI application that analyzes PCAP files using AgentCube code interpreters and LangChain/LangGraph agents.
1095: 
1096: ### Files to Create
1097: 
1098: #### Application
1099: - `example/pcap-analyzer/pcap_analyzer.py`
1100:   - from fastapi import FastAPI, UploadFile, File, Form
1101:   - from pydantic import BaseModel
1102:   - from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
1103:   - from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent
1104:   - from agentcube.code_interpreter import CodeInterpreterClient
1105:   - Config: API_KEY (OPENAI_API_KEY), API_BASE_URL (https://api.siliconflow.cn/v1), MODEL_NAME (Qwen/QwQ-32B), CODEINTERPRETER_NAME, SANDBOX_NAMESPACE, SANDBOX_WARMUP_SEC
1106:   - class SandboxRunner:
1107:     - `__init__(name, namespace, warmup_sec)`
1108:     - `upload_file(local_path, remote_path)` - uses CodeInterpreterClient
1109:     - `upload_bytes(data, remote_path)` - uses CodeInterpreterClient
1110:     - `run(command)` - uses CodeInterpreterClient.execute_command
1111:     - `stop()` - closes CodeInterpreterClient
1112:   - class AnalyzeResponse(BaseModel):

1114:     - results: List[Dict[str, Any]]
1115:     - report: str
1116:   - `build_react_agent(llm, system_prompt)` - creates LangGraph react agent
1117:   - `invoke_react_agent(agent, user_text)` - invokes agent
1118:   - `build_planner_agent(llm)` - creates planning agent

1120:   - `_plan_script(agent, pcap_local_path)` - generates analysis script
1121:   - `_repair_script(agent, prev_script, results)` - repairs script
1122:   - `_execute_once_in_runner(runner, pcap_local_path, script)` - executes script
1123:   - `_analyze_with_retries(..., max_retries=2)` - analyzes with retry loop
1124:   - `_report(agent, results)` - generates report
1125:   - FastAPI app:
1126:     - `@app.on_event("startup")` - builds LLM, PLANNER, REPORTER
1127:     - `@app.post("/analyze")` - analyzes PCAP file or path
1128:       - pcap_file: UploadFile = File(None)

1130:       - response_model: AnalyzeResponse
1131:   - `uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=8000, reload=True)`
1132: 
1133: - `example/pcap-analyzer/requirements.txt`

1135:   - uvicorn==0.38.0
1136:   - langchain==1.0.2

1139:   - langchain-core==1.0.1

1142:   - langgraph==1.0.1
1143:   - langgraph-checkpoint==3.0.0
1144:   - langgraph-prebuilt==1.0.1
1145:   - langgraph-sdk==0.2.9
1146:   - langsmith==0.4.38
1147:   - paramiko==4.0.0
1148:   - python-multipart==0.0.20
1149: 
1150: #### Deployment
1151: - `example/pcap-analyzer/Dockerfile`
1152:   - FROM ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.12-bookworm-slim

1161: 
1162: - `example/pcap-analyzer/deployment.yaml`

1164:     - Namespace: default
1165:     - Container: pcap-analyzer
1166:     - Image: pcap-analyzer:latest
1167:     - ImagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
1168:     - Port: 8000 TCP
1169:     - Resources: requests cpu: 200m, memory: 100Mi; limits cpu: 1, memory: 1Gi
1170:     - Env:
1171:       - OPENAI_API_KEY: SecretRef pcap-analyzer-secrets/openai-api-key
1172:       - OPENAI_API_BASE: https://api.siliconflow.cn/v1
1173:       - OPENAI_MODEL: Qwen/QwQ-32B
1174:       - WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL
1175:       - ROUTER_URL
1176:       - CODEINTERPRETER_NAME: my-interpreter
1177:       - SANDBOX_NAMESPACE: default

1181: 
1182: - `example/pcap-analyzer/README.md` - Usage documentation
1183: 
1184: ---
1185: 
1186: ## Implementation Order
1187: 
1188: The recommended implementation order is:
1189: 
1190: 1. **AR-005 (Sandbox Orchestration)** - Core Kubernetes CRDs and controllers
1191: 2. **AR-004 (Session Store)** - Redis/Valkey persistence layer
1192: 3. **AR-003 (Session Routing)** - Router for traffic routing
1193: 4. **AR-002 (Code Execution)** - PicoD for code execution in sandboxes

state.title: PLAN.md

metadata.preview:
# AgentCube Implementation Plan

## Overview

This document outlines the complete implementation plan for AgentCube, a Kubernetes-native sandbox orchestration platform for AI agents. The implementation is organized into 11 Architectural Requirements (ARs), each representing a major component of the system.

## AR-001: CLI Toolkit

### Purpose
Command-line tool (`kubectl-agentcube`) for packaging, building, publishing, invoking, and checking status of AI agents.

### Files to Create

#### Core Configuration
- `cmd/cli/pyproject.toml` - Project metadata, dependencies, console script mapping
- `cmd/cli/agentcube/__init__.py` - Package version `__version__ = "0.1.0"`, exports `app`, runtimes
- `cmd/cli/agentcube/cli/__init__.py` - CLI subpackage marker

#### CLI Entry Point
- `cmd/cli/agentcube/cli/main.py`

input.content:
# AgentCube Implementation Plan: AR-023 through AR-033

## Overview

This document details the complete implementation of Architectural Requirements AR-023 through AR-033 for the AgentCube Kubernetes-native sandbox orchestration platform. Each AR results in complete, production-ready code with no placeholders or stubs.

## AR-023: CLI Toolkit Implementation

### Purpose
Command-line tool (`kubectl-agentcube`) for packaging, building, publishing, invoking, and checking status of AI agents against AgentCube or standard Kubernetes.

### Files to Create

#### Core Configuration
- **`cmd/cli/pyproject.toml`**
  - Package `agentcube`
  - Dependencies: typer[all]>=0.9.0, pydantic>=2.0.0, pyyaml>=6.0, httpx>=0.24.0, docker>=6.0.0, rich>=13.0.0, packaging>=23.0, importlib-resources>=6.0.0, semver>=3.0.0
  - Optional dependencies: k8s (kubernetes>=28.0.0), dev (pytest>=7.4.0, pytest-asyncio>=0.21.0, pytest-cov>=4.1.0, ruff>=0.1.0, mypy>=1.5.0, pre-commit>=3.3.0, kubernetes>=28.0.0), test (pytest>=7.4.0, pytest-asyncio>=0.21.0, pytest-cov>=4.1.0, pytest-mock>=3.11.0, httpx-mock>=0.10.0)
  - Python >=3.10
  - Console script: `kubectl-agentcube = "agentcube.cli.main:app"`

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/__init__.py`**
  - `__version__ = "0.1.0"`
  - `__author__ = "AgentCube Community"`
  - `__email__ = "agentcube@volcano.sh"`
  - Imports: `app`, `PackRuntime`, `BuildRuntime`, `PublishRuntime`, `InvokeRuntime`, `StatusRuntime`
  - `__all__ = ["app", "PackRuntime", "BuildRuntime", "PublishRuntime", "InvokeRuntime"]`

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/cli/__init__.py`**
  - CLI subpackage marker with docstring

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/cli/main.py`**
  - Typer app: `app = typer.Typer(name="agentcube", help="...", no_args_is_help=True, rich_markup_mode="rich", add_completion=False)`
  - Root callback `main()` with options:
    - `version: Optional[bool]` (eager, callback version_callback, --version, "Show version and exit")
    - `verbose: bool = typer.Option(False, "--verbose", "-v", "Enable verbose output")`
  - `version_callback(value)` - prints `AgentCube CLI (kubectl agentcube) version: {__version__}` and exits with `typer.Exit()`
  - Commands with `--verbose` local flag:
    - `pack(workspace=".", agent_name=None, language=None, entrypoint=None, port=None, build_mode=None, description=None, output=None, verbose=False)`
    - `build(workspace=".", proxy=None, cloud_provider=None, output=None, verbose=False)`
    - `publish(workspace=".", version=None, image_url=None, image_username=None, image_password=None, description=None, region=None, cloud_provider=None, provider="agentcube", node_port=None, replicas=None, namespace=None, verbose=False)`
    - `invoke(workspace=".", payload="{}", header_list=None, provider="agentcube", verbose=False)`
    - `status(workspace=".", provider="agentcube", verbose=False)`
  - `_handle_error(e, command_name, verbose)` - prints red error, optionally traceback with `traceback.print_exc()`, raises `typer.Exit(1)`

#### Data Models
- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/models/pack_models.py`**
  - `MetadataOptions` dataclass with optional fields: agent_name, language, entrypoint, port, build_mode, requirements_file, description, workload_manager_url, router_url, readiness_probe_path, readiness_probe_port, registry_url, registry_username, registry_password, agent_endpoint
  - Defaults: language="python", port=8080, build_mode="local"
  - Class method `from_options(cls, options)` creates instance from dict

#### Runtime Classes
- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/__init__.py`**
  - Re-exports: PackRuntime, BuildRuntime, PublishRuntime, InvokeRuntime, StatusRuntime, MetadataOptions

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/pack_runtime.py`**
  - `PackRuntime` class with `__init__(verbose=False)`
  - `pack(workspace_path, **options)`:
    - Validates workspace with `_validate_workspace_structure`
    - Loads or creates metadata with `_load_or_create_metadata`
    - Applies CLI overrides with `_apply_option_overrides`
    - Validates language compatibility
    - Processes dependencies
    - Optionally generates Dockerfile
    - Updates pack metadata
    - Returns dict with agent_name, workspace_path, metadata_path
  - `_validate_workspace_structure(workspace_path)` - raises ValueError if not directory, Python requires *.py, Java requires pom.xml
  - `_load_or_create_metadata(workspace_path, options)` - loads agent_metadata.yaml with fallback to agent.yaml then metadata.yaml
  - `_apply_option_overrides(metadata, options)` - merges CLI options into metadata
  - `_validate_language_compatibility(workspace_path, metadata)` - delegates to language-specific validation
  - `_validate_python_compatibility(workspace_path)` - checks for *.py files
  - `_validate_java_compatibility(workspace_path)` - checks for pom.xml
  - `_process_dependencies(workspace_path, metadata)` - processes requirements.txt or pom.xml
  - `_process_python_dependencies(workspace_path)` - copies requirements.txt if present
  - `_process_java_dependencies(workspace_path)` - handles pom.xml
  - `_generate_dockerfile(workspace_path, metadata)` - returns path to generated Dockerfile
  - `_generate_python_dockerfile(metadata)` - generates Python Dockerfile content
  - `_generate_java_dockerfile(metadata)` - generates Java Dockerfile content
  - `_update_pack_metadata(workspace_path, metadata, dockerfile_path)` - saves metadata
  - `_prepare_output_path(workspace_path, output_path)` - resolves output directory
  - `_infer_entrypoint(workspace_path, language)` - guesses entrypoint

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/build_runtime.py`**
  - `BuildRuntime` class with `__init__(verbose=False)`
  - `build(workspace_path, **options)`:
    - Validates prerequisites with `_validate_build_prerequisites`
    - Loads metadata
    - Increments version with `_increment_version`
    - Builds local or cloud image
    - Updates build metadata
    - Reverts version on build failure
    - Returns dict with image_name, image_id, image_size, tag
  - `_increment_version(workspace_path, metadata)` - patches version (X.Y.Z+1 or append .1)
  - `_validate_build_prerequisites(workspace_path)` - checks Docker availability
  - `_build_local(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - builds with DockerService.build_image
  - `_build_cloud(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - cloud build logic
  - `_update_build_metadata(workspace_path, metadata, build_result, tag="latest")` - saves image info

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/publish_runtime.py`**
  - `PublishRuntime` class with `__init__(verbose=False, provider="agentcube")`
  - `publish(workspace_path, **options)`:
    - Validates provider and metadata
    - Prepares image with `_prepare_image_for_publishing`
    - Publishes via AgentCubeProvider or KubernetesProvider
    - Waits for deployment ready
    - Updates publish metadata
    - Returns dict with deployment info
  - `_publish_cr_to_k8s(workspace_path, metadata, **options)` - uses AgentCubeProvider.deploy_agent_runtime
  - `_publish_k8s(workspace_path, metadata, **options)` - uses KubernetesProvider.deploy_agent
  - `_prepare_image_for_publishing(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - pushes image or uses explicit URL
  - `_prepare_local_image(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - pushes via DockerService.push_image
  - `_prepare_cloud_image(workspace_path, metadata, options)` - cloud image preparation
  - `_update_publish_metadata(workspace_path, agent_info)` - saves deployment info

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/invoke_runtime.py`**
  - `InvokeRuntime` class with `__init__(verbose=False, provider="agentcube")`
  - `invoke(workspace_path, payload, headers=None)`:
    - Validates with `_validate_invoke_prerequisites`
    - Determines endpoint based on provider and k8s_deployment
    - Rewrites URL for AgentRuntime: {base}/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/agent-runtimes/{agent_name}/invocations/
    - Adds X-Agentcube-Session-Id header if session_id in metadata
    - POSTs JSON payload
    - Returns response
  - `_validate_invoke_prerequisites(workspace_path)` - loads metadata, validates published state
  - `_invoke_agent_via_agentcube(agent_id, payload, headers, endpoint, workspace_path)` - async HTTP POST
  - `_direct_http_invocation(endpoint, payload, headers, workspace_path)` - direct HTTP POST

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/runtime/status_runtime.py`**
  - `StatusRuntime` class with `__init__(verbose=False, provider="agentcube")`
  - `get_status(workspace_path, provider=None)`:
    - Loads metadata
    - Gets status via provider
    - Displays Rich Table
    - Exits with code 1 if not_published or error
    - Returns dict with status info
  - `_get_k8s_status(metadata)` - gets Deployment status via KubernetesProvider
  - `_get_cr_k8s_status(metadata)` - gets AgentRuntime CRD status via AgentCubeProvider

#### Service Classes
- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/services/__init__.py`**
  - Services package marker with docstring

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/services/metadata_service.py`**
  - `AgentMetadata` Pydantic BaseModel with all metadata fields and validators
  - Validators: `validate_language` (python, java), `validate_build_mode` (local, cloud), `validate_port` (1-65535)
  - `MetadataService` class with `__init__(verbose=False)`
  - `load_metadata(workspace_path)` - loads agent_metadata.yaml with fallback
  - `save_metadata(workspace_path, metadata)` - saves to agent_metadata.yaml with yaml.dump
  - `update_metadata(workspace_path, updates)` - updates and saves
  - `validate_workspace(workspace_path)` - validates workspace structure
  - `_validate_python_workspace(workspace_path, metadata)` - Python-specific validation
  - `_validate_java_workspace(workspace_path, metadata)` - Java-specific validation

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/services/docker_service.py`**
  - `DockerService` class with `__init__(verbose=False)`
  - `check_docker_available()` - returns bool
  - `build_image(dockerfile_path, context_path, image_name, tag, build_args)` - builds with tag, returns dict
  - `get_image_info(image_name)` - returns dict with id, size, created
  - `_get_image_info(image_name)` - internal image info retrieval
  - `_format_size(size_bytes)` - formats bytes to human-readable
  - `push_image(image_name, registry_url, username, password)` - pushes image, returns dict
  - `_docker_login_sdk(registry, username, password)` - SDK login
  - `_tag_image_sdk(source_image, target_image)` - tags image
  - `remove_image(image_name)` - returns bool, logs warning on failure

- **`cmd/cli/agentcube/services/k8s_provider.py`**
  - `KubernetesProvider` class with `__init__(namespace="default", verbose=False, kubeconfig=None)`
  - `_ensure_namespace()` - creates namespace if missing
  - `deploy_agent(agent_name, image_url, port, entrypoint=None, replicas=1, node_port=None, env_vars=None)`:
    - Creates Deployment and NodePort Service
    - Waits for rollout with `wait_for_deployment_ready`
    - Returns dict with deployment_name, service_name, namespace, replicas, container_port, node_port, service_url
  - `_create_deployment(name, image_url, port, entrypoint, replicas, env_vars)` - returns Deployment
  - `_create_service(name, port, node_port)` - returns Service
